etymology.description: semantic 骨 (gǔ, "bone") + phonetic 豊 (lǐ) -> representation of the body or form
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 骨 (骨) (gǔ, "bone") serving as the semantic component to indicate something related to the physical structure or skeleton, and 豊 (lǐ) as the phonetic component to provide the sound. This combination suggests something pertaining to the body, emphasizing the underlying structure.
etymology.simplification: 体 (體) simplifies by reducing the complex 骨 (骨) (gǔ, "bone") component to 亻(亻) (rén, "person") for ease of writing, and replaces 豊 (lǐ) with 本 (běn, "basis") to retain a semblance of pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 每天锻炼可以保持健康的身体(每天鍛鍊可以保持健康的身體)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān duànliàn kěyǐ bǎochí jiànkāng de shēntǐ.
examples.english: Exercising daily can keep a healthy body.

examples.chinese: 书法有很多种字体(書法有很多種字體)，每种都有独特的风格(每種都有獨特的風格)。
examples.pinyin: Shūfǎ yǒu hěn duō zhǒng zìtǐ, měi zhǒng dōu yǒu dútè de fēnggé.
examples.english: There are many styles of calligraphy, each with a unique style.

examples.chinese: 他的写作风格非常特别(他的寫作風格非常特別)，我非常喜欢(我非常喜歡)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiězuò fēnggé fēicháng tèbié, wǒ fēicháng xǐhuān.
examples.english: His writing style is very unique, and I like it a lot.
